column4: card's own ability requires being in play/ready; obvious a burned/ash-heaped card can't act — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.5, adds nothing new.
'X or X antitribu' clan requirement; 'or' meaning either is plain-English obvious — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.6.
restates the card's own printed target (prey); no general targeting principle beyond this card's text — fails transfer. Nearest 3.1.
confirms the chosen card is visible to all; ash heaps are already fully public zones, so this is obvious — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.11.
cannot burn a vampire no longer in play; obvious you cannot affect what isn't there — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.10.
bidding/auction resolution: no section owns how pool moves between bid participants (winner pays, losers keep pool); nearest 6.5, which doesn't claim auction procedure.
bidding/auction validity: no section owns minimum-bid requirements for auction-type cards; nearest 6.5, which doesn't claim auction procedure.

sect-as-trait gap (watch list): nothing owns a temporary sect override's precedence over a sect-change card being played; nearest 5.8 (sect effects on titles) and 5.7 (sect-derived traits), neither claims the override mechanism itself.

sect-as-trait gap (watch list) — core override-precedence rule (cf. Galaric's Legacy.1): most recent override wins, forces Independent rather than the blocked sect, underlying sect returns when the override ends; nearest 5.8,5.7.
sect-as-trait gap (watch list): an 'impersonator' bypasses its own current sect for card-requirement purposes; nearest 5.8,5.7, neither owns the general bypass mechanism.
sect-as-trait gap (watch list), Sabbat mirror of Fall of the Camarilla.1; nearest 5.8,5.7.
sect-as-trait gap (watch list), Sabbat mirror of the core override-precedence rule (cf. Galaric's Legacy.1); nearest 5.8,5.7.
sect-as-trait gap (watch list), Sabbat mirror of the impersonator/bypass ruling; nearest 5.8,5.7.
blocks a stealth-then-cancel combo unique to this card's own ability chaining into itself — fails transfer, affects no other card. Nearest 3.2.
obviously cannot steal a weapon that doesn't exist; restates a precondition already implied by the card's own text — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 4.10.
sect-as-trait gap (watch list), same override-precedence principle as Fall of the Camarilla/Sabbat.2 (cf. Galaric's Legacy.1); nearest 5.8,5.7.
restates that the acting vampire must have the discipline the card's own text requires to choose from — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.6.

cumulative/stacking effects gap (watch list, cf. Betrayer.3): nothing owns whether multiple copies' periodic burn conditions stack per-copy; nearest 1.2, which owns periodicity templates but not stacking of copies.
ordering between Follow the Blood and Perfectionist named by card — fails transfer, depends on this exact pairing rather than a reusable rule. Nearest 2.4.
!TENSION:no-effect-plays permission side: usable/unlocks despite the action having no effect from a missing suitable target (cf. Draba.3).
